items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Ah... it's all gone now. But I'll ask the tavern keeper if he can prepare something for you later.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The Laurus Salad was as delightful as ever. Well worth reacquainting myself with so I can make a bowl myself.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Fresh, crisp, sweet... Heh.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Come, sit with me, {PlayerName}. Let's enjoy a moment of peace.

options.text: It seems your sense of taste has returned then?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Yes, and so... I've been thinking about what I vowed to you before.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: To savor the things I desire, visit the places I long for, and meet those I wish to see.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: To become a roving knight is my true ambition. A wish list is something to be experienced one step at a time.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I'd say your first wish has already been fulfilled.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The third, too... though by unexpected means.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Those are my adoptive parents... and their child.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Perhaps it was fate's cruel jest. I couldn't find any trace of them after they moved from Ragunna, but here in Septimont, I discovered where they had resettled.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Only... they no longer recognize me.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The truth that I was their daughter was a memory the Threnodian crafted for them, disappearing when It was sealed away in the tower.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I don't know what they went through after that fateful day, but at least... they are still alive. They even have their own child.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: They walk here every day, so I come and sit for a while to watch them from a distance.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Cartethyia...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: It's alright, {PlayerName}. In fact, I'm at peace with it now.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: If they had died because of the Order's heavy hand, I would've felt true pain. But this... this is the best outcome I could hope for.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: To live twenty years with the weight of a lost child... I don't want to imagine that kind of torment.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Besides, through all these twists of fate, all three of my wishes have been fulfilled.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Now if I crave a Laurus Salad, I can prepare it myself and savor its taste.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: And I still have many years ahead to see the world and the places I long to visit.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: As for the people I hope to meet... this unexpected journey led me to find them in another city.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The winds of Rinascita always seem to bring me what I seek. I am content.
